calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Continued To Apr-26-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS RESO HOLDINGS, INC. AND SUZANNE FELSON'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is granted. The delegation clause is enforceable and thus the arbitrator will determine all arbitrability issues. The phrase "worksite employer" clearly refers to Reso Holdings, Inc., who is an intended third party beneficiary of the DRP. There is no ambiguity. Paragraph 9c provides that the arbitration will be in accordance with the JAMS or AAA rules. Both sets of rules provide that the arbitrator decides arbitrability. The reference to those rules is clear and unmistakable. (Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557 ("The Contract provides that ' it must be submitted to binding arbitration in accordance with the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. These rules specify that the arbitrator will decide disputes over the scope of the arbitration agreement. We conclude that the parties' agreement to arbitrate according to this rule is clear and unmistakable evidence of the intent that the arbitrator will decide whether a Contested Claim is arbitrable.").) The failure to attach the rules is of no consequence because plaintiff could have easily located them online. (Lane v. Francis Captial Management, LLC (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 676, 691 ("[W]e conclude the failure to attach a copy of the AAA rules did not render the agreement procedurally unconscionable. There could be no surprise, as the arbitration rules referenced in the agreement were easily accessible to the parties-the AAA rules are available on the Internet").) While the delegation clause is procedurally unconscionable because it was presented in a take it or leave it manner and not conspicuous in the TCA (Pinela v. Neiman Marcus Group, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 227, 243), plaintiff has not shown that it is substantively unconscionable. Unlike Pinela, there is no choice of law issue. Nor is the delegation clause one-sided or any other indicia of substantive unconscionability. Pinela, 238 Cal.App.4th at 245 provides that "clear delegation clauses in employment arbitration agreements are substantively unconscionable only if they impose unfair or one-sided burdens that are different from the clauses' inherent features and consequences."(emphasis in original). Those circumstances are not present here. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR # 6576 =302/hk
